{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VV5rosKPELGQnGKyEXGE5P","type":"tool_result","content":"13-## Abstract\n14-\n15-**Purpose:** \n16:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy with methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and methionine synthase reductase (MTRR) polymorphisms.\n17-\n18-**Patients and Methods:** \n19-In the study, a population of 32 patients in East China with early RA fulfilling the diagnostic standards of the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) were enrolled, all of them received MTX monotherapy. Genotyping of patients MTHFR C677T and A1298C, MTRR A66G using tetra-primer ARMS-PCR method and sanger sequencing to verify its accuracy.\n--\n24-**Conclusion:** \n25-Our findings suggest that the MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms may not predict MTX clinical treatment response and disease activity in patients with early RA. The study revealed that smoke, alcohol, and males were possible influential factors for MTX non-response.\n26-\n27:Keywords: MTHFR, MTRR, methotrexate, rheumatoid arthritis, DAS28\n28-\n29-### Purpose\n30-\n31:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B2xZqwfvFSk5Ly9Czgch8G","type":"tool_result","content":"1:# MTHFR and MTRR Genetic Polymorphism of Methotrexate Therapy Outcomes in Early Rheumatoid Arthritis\n2-\n3-## Metadata\n4-**Authors:** Qian Zhang, Pan Fu, Zhanglei Cao, Hua Huang, Qinwen Wen, Kaizhe Wang, Tong Kong, Xiudi Wu, Jianping Zheng\n5-**Journal:** Pharmacogenomics and Personalized Medicine\n6-**Date:** 2023 May 2\n--\n11-**PDF:** [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10163902/pdf/pgpm-16-407.pdf](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10163902/pdf/pgpm-16-407.pdf)\n12-\n13-## Abstract\n14-\n15-**Purpose:** \n16:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy with methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and methionine synthase reductase (MTRR) polymorphisms.\n17-\n18-**Patients and Methods:** \n19:In the study, a population of 32 patients in East China with early RA fulfilling the diagnostic standards of the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) were enrolled, all of them received MTX monotherapy. Genotyping of patients MTHFR C677T and A1298C, MTRR A66G using tetra-primer ARMS-PCR method and sanger sequencing to verify its accuracy.\n20-\n21-**Results:** \n22-[Omitted long context line]\n23-\n24-**Conclusion:** \n25:Our findings suggest that the MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms may not predict MTX clinical treatment response and disease activity in patients with early RA. The study revealed that smoke, alcohol, and males were possible influential factors for MTX non-response.\n26-\n27:Keywords: MTHFR, MTRR, methotrexate, rheumatoid arthritis, DAS28\n28-\n29-### Purpose\n30-\n31:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy with methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and methionine synthase reductase (MTRR) polymorphisms.\n32-\n33-### Patients and Methods\n34-\n35:In the study, a population of 32 patients in East China with early RA fulfilling the diagnostic standards of the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) were enrolled, all of them received MTX monotherapy. Genotyping of patients MTHFR C677T and A1298C, MTRR A66G using tetra-primer ARMS-PCR method and sanger sequencing to verify its accuracy.\n36-\n37-### Results\n38-\n39-[Omitted long context line]\n40-\n41-### Conclusion\n42-\n43:Our findings suggest that the MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms may not predict MTX clinical treatment response and disease activity in patients with early RA. The study revealed that smoke, alcohol, and males were possible influential factors for MTX non-response.\n44-\n45-## Introduction\n46-\n47-[Omitted long context line]\n48-\n49:[Omitted long matching line]\n50-\n51:[Omitted long matching line]\n52-\n53:Therefore, in this study, we investigated a potential correlation between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy and MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms in RA patients in East China for the first time, hoping to provide a possibility to achieve personalized treatment for RA patients.\n54-\n55-## Materials and Methods\n56-\n57-### Workflow Chart\n58-\n--\n62-\n63-![Figure 1](https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/7467/10163902/d1d2c4992489/PGPM-16-407-g0001.jpg)\n64-\n65-[View larger image](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/core/lw/2.0/html/tileshop_pmc/tileshop_pmc_inline.html?title=Click%20on%20image%20to%20zoom&p=PMC3&id=10163902_PGPM-16-407-g0001.jpg)\n66-\n67:Workflow chart of the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of methotrexate (MTX) monotherapy with MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G SNPs polymorphisms.\n68-\n69-### Characteristics of the Population\n70-\n71-[Omitted long context line]\n72-\n--\n74-\n75-[Omitted long context line]\n76-\n77-### Genotype Identification\n78-\n79:[Omitted long matching line]\n80-\n81-### Meta-Analysis\n82-\n83:[Omitted long matching line]\n84-\n85-### Statistical Analysis\n86-\n87-[Omitted long context line]\n88-\n89:Meta-analysis was conducted using STATA 17.0 (Stata Corporation, TX, USA) software. The Mantel-Haenszel test was used to statistically analyze the genetic models such as dominant model, recessive model and co-dominant model to determine the correlation between MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms and MTX treatment efficacy. Risk estimates were expressed using OR and 95% CIs, and *Z*-test P values <0.05 were considered statistically significant.\n90-\n91-## Results\n92-\n93-### Patient Genotype\n94-\n95:[Omitted long matching line]\n96-\n97-### Figure 2.\n98-\n99-![Figure 2](https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/7467/10163902/92d440c6b4f0/PGPM-16-407-g0002.jpg)\n100-\n101-[View larger image](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/core/lw/2.0/html/tileshop_pmc/tileshop_pmc_inline.html?title=Click%20on%20image%20to%20zoom&p=PMC3&id=10163902_PGPM-16-407-g0002.jpg)\n102-\n103:[Omitted long matching line]\n104-\n105-### Clinical Features of the Patients\n106-\n107-[Omitted long context line]\n108-\n--\n131-| ESR, median (IQR) | 19.5 (10–33) | 19 (9–28) | 23 (10–67) |   | 0.330 |\n132-| DAS 28 (mean ± SD) | 3.12 ± 1.55 | 2.21 ± 0.70 | 3.93 ± 1.6 |   | 0.061 |\n133-\n134-Table 1 Caption: Clinical Characteristics Variables of the RA Patients Studied\n135-\n136:### Association of MTX Clinical Response with C677T, A1298C and A66G Polymorphisms\n137-\n138:[Table 2](#t0002) shows the distribution of MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphism genotypes between the response and non-response groups and comparison of statistical models. The results indicated that the genotypes, alleles and the distribution of the three statistical models (recessive model, dominant model and co-dominant model) at each SNP site were not significantly correlated with MTX clinical treatment response.\n139-\n140-### Table 2.\n141-\n142-|   | Responders n (%) | Non-Respondersn (%) | P-value | Compared Model | P-value | OR (95% CI) |\n143-| --- | --- | MTHFR and MTRR Genetic Polymorphism of Methotrexate Therapy Outcomes in Early Rheumatoid Arthritis\n2-\n3-## Metadata\n4-**Authors:** Qian Zhang, Pan Fu, Zhanglei Cao, Hua Huang, Qinwen Wen, Kaizhe Wang, Tong Kong, Xiudi Wu, Jianping Zheng\n5-**Journal:** Pharmacogenomics and Personalized Medicine\n6-**Date:** 2023 May 2\n--\n11-**PDF:** [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10163902/pdf/pgpm-16-407.pdf](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10163902/pdf/pgpm-16-407.pdf)\n12-\n13-## Abstract\n14-\n15-**Purpose:** \n16:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy with methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and methionine synthase reductase (MTRR) polymorphisms.\n17-\n18-**Patients and Methods:** \n19:In the study, a population of 32 patients in East China with early RA fulfilling the diagnostic standards of the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) were enrolled, all of them received MTX monotherapy. Genotyping of patients MTHFR C677T and A1298C, MTRR A66G using tetra-primer ARMS-PCR method and sanger sequencing to verify its accuracy.\n20-\n21-**Results:** \n22-[Omitted long context line]\n23-\n24-**Conclusion:** \n25:Our findings suggest that the MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms may not predict MTX clinical treatment response and disease activity in patients with early RA. The study revealed that smoke, alcohol, and males were possible influential factors for MTX non-response.\n26-\n27:Keywords: MTHFR, MTRR, methotrexate, rheumatoid arthritis, DAS28\n28-\n29-### Purpose\n30-\n31:Methotrexate (MTX) is used as an anchor drug for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and there may be differences in drug action between genotypes. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy with methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and methionine synthase reductase (MTRR) polymorphisms.\n32-\n33-### Patients and Methods\n34-\n35:In the study, a population of 32 patients in East China with early RA fulfilling the diagnostic standards of the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) were enrolled, all of them received MTX monotherapy. Genotyping of patients MTHFR C677T and A1298C, MTRR A66G using tetra-primer ARMS-PCR method and sanger sequencing to verify its accuracy.\n36-\n37-### Results\n38-\n39-[Omitted long context line]\n40-\n41-### Conclusion\n42-\n43:Our findings suggest that the MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms may not predict MTX clinical treatment response and disease activity in patients with early RA. The study revealed that smoke, alcohol, and males were possible influential factors for MTX non-response.\n44-\n45-## Introduction\n46-\n47-[Omitted long context line]\n48-\n49:[Omitted long matching line]\n50-\n51:[Omitted long matching line]\n52-\n53:Therefore, in this study, we investigated a potential correlation between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of MTX monotherapy and MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms in RA patients in East China for the first time, hoping to provide a possibility to achieve personalized treatment for RA patients.\n54-\n55-## Materials and Methods\n56-\n57-### Workflow Chart\n58-\n--\n62-\n63-![Figure 1](https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/7467/10163902/d1d2c4992489/PGPM-16-407-g0001.jpg)\n64-\n65-[View larger image](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/core/lw/2.0/html/tileshop_pmc/tileshop_pmc_inline.html?title=Click%20on%20image%20to%20zoom&p=PMC3&id=10163902_PGPM-16-407-g0001.jpg)\n66-\n67:Workflow chart of the relationship between clinical efficacy response and disease activity of methotrexate (MTX) monotherapy with MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G SNPs polymorphisms.\n68-\n69-### Characteristics of the Population\n70-\n71-[Omitted long context line]\n72-\n--\n74-\n75-[Omitted long context line]\n76-\n77-### Genotype Identification\n78-\n79:[Omitted long matching line]\n80-\n81-### Meta-Analysis\n82-\n83:[Omitted long matching line]\n84-\n85-### Statistical Analysis\n86-\n87-[Omitted long context line]\n88-\n89:Meta-analysis was conducted using STATA 17.0 (Stata Corporation, TX, USA) software. The Mantel-Haenszel test was used to statistically analyze the genetic models such as dominant model, recessive model and co-dominant model to determine the correlation between MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms and MTX treatment efficacy. Risk estimates were expressed using OR and 95% CIs, and *Z*-test P values <0.05 were considered statistically significant.\n90-\n91-## Results\n92-\n93-### Patient Genotype\n94-\n95:[Omitted long matching line]\n96-\n97-### Figure 2.\n98-\n99-![Figure 2](https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/7467/10163902/92d440c6b4f0/PGPM-16-407-g0002.jpg)\n100-\n101-[View larger image](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/core/lw/2.0/html/tileshop_pmc/tileshop_pmc_inline.html?title=Click%20on%20image%20to%20zoom&p=PMC3&id=10163902_PGPM-16-407-g0002.jpg)\n102-\n103:[Omitted long matching line]\n104-\n105-### Clinical Features of the Patients\n106-\n107-[Omitted long context line]\n108-\n--\n131-| ESR, median (IQR) | 19.5 (10–33) | 19 (9–28) | 23 (10–67) |   | 0.330 |\n132-| DAS 28 (mean ± SD) | 3.12 ± 1.55 | 2.21 ± 0.70 | 3.93 ± 1.6 |   | 0.061 |\n133-\n134-Table 1 Caption: Clinical Characteristics Variables of the RA Patients Studied\n135-\n136:### Association of MTX Clinical Response with C677T, A1298C and A66G Polymorphisms\n137-\n138:[Table 2](#t0002) shows the distribution of MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphism genotypes between the response and non-response groups and comparison of statistical models. The results indicated that the genotypes, alleles and the distribution of the three statistical models (recessive model, dominant model and co-dominant model) at each SNP site were not significantly correlated with MTX clinical treatment response.\n139-\n140-### Table 2.\n141-\n142-|   | Responders n (%) | Non-Respondersn (%) | P-value | Compared Model | P-value | OR (95% CI) |\n143-| --- | --- | 169→\n   170→To determine the relationship between different genotypes and disease activity in patients, the distribution between MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G polymorphisms genotypes and disease activity parameters were compared, as shown in [Table 3](#t0003). The results showed that ESR levels were not remarkably different across the C677T genotypes and were reduced in the 1298AC and 66GG genotypes (P > 0.05). DAS28 was decreased after the post-treatment in 677TT and 1298AC, but was not statistically significant. TJC and SJC were not found to be significantly different across genotypes in the three SNPs. To further compare the differences in each disease activity parameter between the response and non-response groups at baseline and after the post-treatment, [Figure S1](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx) shows that ESR, TJC, SJC and DAS28 were remarkably lower in the response group (p < 0.001), while the differences were not statistically significant in the non-response group.\n   171→\n   172→### Table 3.\n   173→\n   174→|   | ESR |  | TJC |  | SJC |  | DAS28 |  |\n   175→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n   176→|   | Mean ± SD | K-W | Median (IQR) | K-W | Median (IQR) | K-W | Mean ± SD | K-W |\n   177→| MTHFR C677T genotype |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |\n   178→| CC | 26.45 ± 24.65 | 0.302 | 1 (0–2) | 0.867 | 1 (0–1) | 0.868 | 3.03 ± 1.23 | 0.975 |\n   179→| CT | 26.73 ± 20.20 | 0.302 | 1 (0–2) | 0.867 | 0 (0–1) | 0.868 | 3.24 ± 1.50 | 0.975 |\n   180→| TT | 22.17 ± 14.88 | 0.302 | 1 (0–5) | 0.867 | 0 (0–2) | 0.868 | 2.98 ± 1.03 | 0.975 |\n   181→| MTHFR A1298C genotype |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |\n   182→| AA | 30.41 ± 21.95 | 0.056 | 1 (0–2) | 1 | 0 (0–2) | 0.571 | 3.35 ± 1.42 | 0.186 |\n   183→| AC | 15.60 ± 12.34 | 0.056 | 1.5 (0–2) | 1 | 0 (0–1) | 0.571 | 2.61 ± 0.66 | 0.186 |\n   184→| MTRR A66G genotype |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |\n   185→| AA | 26.52 ± 20.80 | 0.766 | 1 (0–2) | 0.666 | 0 (0–1) | 0.591 | 3.10 ± 1.36 | 0.883 |\n   186→| AG | 25.50 ± 21.51 | 0.766 | 1 (0–2) | 0.666 | 0.5 (0–1) | 0.591 | 3.14 ± 1.22 | 0.883 |\n   187→| GG | 13 | 0.766 | 2 | 0.666 | 1 | 0.591 | 3.26 | 0.883 |\n   188→\n   189→Table 3 Caption: Disease Activity Parameters Related to Polymorphic Genotypes\n   190→\n   191→### Meta-Analysis\n   192→\n   193→A total of 373 publications were identified after the initial search, and 37 publications were included after analysis of titles and abstracts. The full literature was interpreted in detail, and 13 of these publications met our inclusion criteria, with reasons for exclusion: (1) no detailed data for genotype; (2) letter or comment.\n   194→\n   195→MTHFR C677T: The meta-analysis of MTHFR C677T included 12 studies,[19–30](#cit0019) which included 958 responders and 840 non-responders, and the main characteristic information is presented in [Table 4](#t0004). For all samples, dominant model (OR = 1.095, 95% CI = 0.81–1.362, P = 0.412) ([Figure 3](#f0003)), recessive model (OR = 0.701, 95% CI = 0.81–1.362, P = 0.087) ([Figure S2A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.059, 95% CI = 0.844–1.329, P = 0.621) ([Figure S2B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) did not observe an association between the C677T polymorphism and MTX treatment response, nor was significant heterogeneity observed between studies. Study cohort stratified by ethnicity found that C677T polymorphism was significantly associated with MTX treatment response in the recessive model (OR = 0.497, 95% CI = 0.279–0.886, P = 0.018) and codominant model (OR = 1.492, 95% CI = 1.046–2.126, P = 0.027) in the European population, no association was found in other models and populations ([Table 5](#t0005)).\n   196→\n   197→### Table 4.\n For all samples, dominant model (OR = 1.095, 95% CI = 0.81–1.362, P = 0.412) ([Figure 3](#f0003)), recessive model (OR = 0.701, 95% CI = 0.81–1.362, P = 0.087) ([Figure S2A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.059, 95% CI = 0.844–1.329, P = 0.621) ([Figure S2B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) did not observe an association between the C677T polymorphism and MTX treatment response, nor was significant heterogeneity observed between studies. | - |\n   285→| Overall | 3 | 1.177 (0.850–1.629) | 0.327 | 0 | 0.389 |\n   286→\n   287→Table 5 Caption: Association of MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C and MTRR A66G Gene Polymorphisms with the Outcome of MTX in RA Patients\n   288→\n   289→MTHFR A1298C: The meta-analysis of MTHFR A1298C included 9 studies,[20–24](#cit0020)^,^[26–29](#cit0026) which included 688 responders and 615 non-responders, and the main characteristic information is presented in [Table 6](#t0006). For all samples, dominant model (OR = 1.023, 95% CI = 0.730–1.435, P = 0.894) ([Figure S3A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)), recessive model (OR = 0.784, 95% CI = 0.390–1.578, P = 0.495) ([Figure S3B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.075, 95% CI = 0.760–1.520, P = 0.682) ([Figure 4](#f0004)) did not observe an association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response. The study cohort was stratified by ethnicity, and there was a significant association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response in the recessive model (OR = 0.432, 95% CI = 0.201–0.926, P = 0.031) and the codominant model (OR = 1.981, 95% CI=0.1.108–3.543, P = 0.021) in the South Asian population. In addition, there was significant between study heterogeneity in the recessive model (I^2^=55%, P = 0.023) ([Table 5](#t0005)).\n   290→\n   291→### Table 6.\n   292→\n   293→| Study | Year | Abbreviations: OR, odds ratio; Cis, confidence interval.\n   315→\n   316→MTRR A66G: The meta-analysis of MTRR A66G included 3 studies,[21](#cit0021)^,^[28](#cit0028)^,^[31](#cit0031) which included 376 responders and 338 non-responders, and the main characteristic information is presented in [Table 7](#t0007). For all samples, dominant model (OR = 0.879, 95% CI = 0.619–1.249, P = 0.473) ([Figure S4A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)), recessive model (OR = 0.910, 95% CI = 0.594–1.392, P = 0.663) ([Figure S4B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.177, 95% CI = 0.850–1.629, P = 0.327) ([Figure 5](#f0005)) did not observe an association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response. No significant heterogeneity between studies was observed in any of the three genetic models ([Table 5](#t0005)).\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. For all samples, dominant model (OR = 1.023, 95% CI = 0.730–1.435, P = 0.894) ([Figure S3A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)), recessive model (OR = 0.784, 95% CI = 0.390–1.578, P = 0.495) ([Figure S3B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.075, 95% CI = 0.760–1.520, P = 0.682) ([Figure 4](#f0004)) did not observe an association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response. The study cohort was stratified by ethnicity, and there was a significant association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response in the recessive model (OR = 0.432, 95% CI = 0.201–0.926, P = 0.031) and the codominant model (OR = 1.981, 95% CI=0.1.108–3.543, P = 0.021) in the South Asian population. In addition, there was significant between study heterogeneity in the recessive model (I^2^=55%, P = 0.023) ([Table 5](#t0005)).\n\n### Table 6.\n\n| Study | Year | Ethnicity | Genotyping Method | For all samples, dominant model (OR = 0.879, 95% CI = 0.619–1.249, P = 0.473) ([Figure S4A](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)), recessive model (OR = 0.910, 95% CI = 0.594–1.392, P = 0.663) ([Figure S4B](https://www.dovepress.com/get_supplementary_file.php?f=404949.docx)) and codominant model (OR = 1.177, 95% CI = 0.850–1.629, P = 0.327) ([Figure 5](#f0005)) did not observe an association between the A1298C polymorphism and MTX treatment response. No significant heterogeneity between studies was observed in any of the three genetic models ([Table 5](#t0005)).","numLines":80,"startLine":237,"totalLines":460}}}
